    <p>{`Use the following steps to verify and test your entitlement to pull IBM MQ certified containers from the IBM Cloud Container Registry.`}</p>
    <InlineNotification mdxType="InlineNotification">
      <p>{`If you do not have an Entitlement key or if it fails to pull the IBM MQ docker image, please contact your IBM representative.`}</p>
    </InlineNotification>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li">{`Ensure `}<a parentName="p" {...{
            "href": "/curam-kubernetes/prereq/docker-helm"
          }}>{`Docker`}</a>{` is installed and running in the local workstation.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li">{`Log into `}<a parentName="p" {...{
            "href": "https://myibm.ibm.com/products-services/containerlibrary"
          }}>{`My IBM Container Software Library`}</a>{` with the IBM ID and password that are associated with the entitled software.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li">{`In the `}<strong parentName="p">{`Entitlement keys`}</strong>{` section, select `}<strong parentName="p">{`Copy key`}</strong>{` to copy the entitlement key to the clipboard.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li">{`Log into IBM Cloud Container Registry by running the following command. Replace `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<MY_ENTITLEMENT_KEY>`}</inlineCode>{` with your key. A message `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Login Succeeded`}</inlineCode>{` should be displayed if authentication is succeful.`}</p>
        <pre parentName="li"><code parentName="pre" {...{}}>{`docker login cp.icr.io --username cp --password <MY_ENTITLEMENT_KEY>
`}</code></pre>

        <p parentName="li">{`Pull the IBM MQ docker image.`}</p>
        <pre parentName="li"><code parentName="pre" {...{}}>{`docker pull cp.icr.io/cp/ibm-mqadvanced-server:9.4.3.0-r2
`}</code></pre>
        <p parentName="li">{`The following output should be displayed:`}</p>
        <pre parentName="li"><code parentName="pre" {...{}}>{`9.4.3.0-r2: Pulling from cp/ibm-mqadvanced-server

`}</code></pre>

`}</code></pre>

    <h2>{`IBM MQ containers on AKS and EKS`}</h2>
    <p>{`IBM MQ containers are not limited to OpenShift deployments.`}<br parentName="p"></br>{`
`}{`They are also fully supported and production-ready on `}<strong parentName="p">{`Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)`}</strong>{` and `}<strong parentName="p">{`Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)`}</strong>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`This extended support allows organizations to deploy IBM MQ in a broader range of managed Kubernetes environments, enabling greater flexibility when designing cloud-native architectures.`}<br parentName="p"></br>{`
`}{`The same operational capabilities, container images, and configuration principles apply across OCP, EKS, and AKS, ensuring consistent behaviour regardless of the chosen platform.`}</p>
    <h2>{`IBM MQ containers on OpenShift`}</h2>
    <p>{`Cúram does not require or support the use of any IBM MQ Advanced features available in the IBM MQ certified containers.`}</p>
    <p>{`IBM MQ certified container is supported only from Cúram Version 7.0.11 and later versions.`}</p>
    <InlineNotification mdxType="InlineNotification">
      <p><strong parentName="p">{`Note`}</strong>{`: The IBM MQ charts found in the Runbook Github repository are sample charts based on the IBM MQ public charts published in the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
          "href": "https://github.com/IBM/charts/tree/master/stable/ibm-mqadvanced-server-dev"
        }}>{`IBM MQ charts`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    </InlineNotification>
    <h2>{`Operators`}</h2>
    <p>{`MQ can also be deployed via Operators. Operators are a method of packaging and deploying Kubernetes applications that take human operational knowledge of managing the application and encode it into the package itself,
which can then be shared with users. Operator deployment can be used to create a single-instance or a multi-instance deployment, as desired.
The `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/9.3?topic=containers-support-mq-operator"
      }}>{`IBM MQ Operator`}</a>{` can be installed using the Operator Hub. More information on  installing the IBM Operator using the OpenShift CLI
can be found in the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/9.1?topic=openshift-installing-mq-operator-using-cli"
      }}>{`OpenShift Cli`}</a>{` documentation.
When deploying in this way, a `}<strong parentName="p">{`Queue Manager`}</strong>{` object is created.`}</p>
    <p>{`MQ Operators used in Cúram requires the values for `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`availabilityType`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`storageType`}</inlineCode>{`, and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`storageClassName`}</inlineCode>{` to be configured prior to deployment.
These values are those located under the MQ `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`global.mq`}</inlineCode>{` section within the relevant values file.`}</p>
    <h3>{`MQ File Systems Consideration`}</h3>
    <p>{`IBM MQ provides guidance on the functional capabilities for a file system and the testing a user should complete in their own environment.`}</p>
    <p>{`For more information, please see the following documentation on `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/testing-statement-ibm-mq-multi-instance-queue-manager-file-systems"
      }}>{`IBM MQ multi-instance queue manager file systems`}</a>{`,
`}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/ibm-mq-considerations-efs-aws"
      }}>{`IBM MQ considerations for EFS on AWS`}</a>{` and
`}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://access.redhat.com/solutions/4591701"
      }}>{`Red Hat OpenShift support for AWS EFS`}</a>{`.`}</p>
